babyLearning.en: stretching and kicking, settling into a rhythm
babyLearning.hi: tan kar laaton maar raha hai, ek laya mein bas raha hai

title.hi: Tum Kahan Se Aaye Ho, Yeh Maayne Rakhta Hai

insight.en: Your baby is not arriving into an empty world. They are joining a family with a history, a warmth and a story already waiting.
insight.hi: Aapka baby kisi khaali duniya mein nahi aa raha. Woh ek aise parivaar mein shaamil ho raha hai jiska itihaas, garmjoshi aur kahani pehle se intezaar kar rahe hain.

expanded.en: In these last weeks, it helps to remember that you are not raising your child alone or from scratch. Behind you stands a long line of people who loved, struggled and carried on — and your baby inherits all of it. Knowing where they come from gives a child something quiet and strong to stand on. You do not have to make their world perfect; you only have to let them feel held by something larger than themselves. The family they are born into is their first and softest ground.
expanded.hi: In aakhri hafton mein, yeh yaad rakhna madad karta hai ki aap apne bachche ko akele ya bilkul shuruaat se nahi paal rahin. Aapke peeche logon ki ek lambi kataar khadi hai jinhone pyaar kiya, sangharsh kiya aur aage badhe — aur aapka baby yeh sab virasat mein paata hai. Yeh jaanna ki woh kahan se aaye hain, ek bachche ko kuch shaant aur mazboot deta hai jis par woh khada ho sake. Aapko unki duniya perfect banane ki zaroorat nahi; aapko bas unhe khud se bade kisi cheez se thaame hua mehsoos karaana hai. Jis parivaar mein woh janm lete hain, wahi unki pehli aur sabse naram zameen hai.

deepDive.en: Studies of family storytelling find that children who know their family's history — its ups and downs — tend to feel more resilient and rooted. A sense of belonging acts like an emotional anchor.
deepDive.hi: Family storytelling par hui studies paati hain ki jo bachche apne parivaar ka itihaas jaante hain — uske utaar-chadhaav — woh zyada lachile aur judey hue mehsoos karte hain. Apnepan ki bhaavna ek emotional anchor ki tarah kaam karti hai.

remember.en: Your baby is born already belonging to someone.
remember.hi: Aapka baby janm se hi kisi ka hokar paida hota hai.

summary.en: A small acorn learns it already carries a whole forest within.
summary.hi: Ek chhota balloot seekhta hai ki uske andar pehle se ek poora jungle bharaa hai.

body.en:
High on a great oak lived a little acorn who felt very small. All around stood tall trees with spreading branches, while the acorn was no bigger than a thumb. 'I am too tiny to matter,' it sighed, looking down at the wide world below.

One autumn day the acorn fell softly to the earth and rolled into the cool soil. There an old root whispered to it from deep underground. 'Little one, do not measure yourself by your size. Inside you sleeps an entire forest — every branch, every leaf, every acorn yet to come.'

The acorn could hardly believe it. But through the quiet winter it rested, and in spring a tender green shoot pushed up toward the light. Slowly, year by year, it grew. And one day it stood tall and broad, dropping acorns of its own. The little seed had carried its whole family inside it all along.
body.hi:
Ek bade oak ke ped par ooper ek chhota balloot rehta tha jo khud ko bahut chhota mehsoos karta. Chaaron taraf phaili tehniyon wale ooche ped khade the, jabki balloot ek angoothe se bada nahi tha. 'Main itna nanha hoon ki maayne hi nahi rakhta,' usne aah bhari, neeche ki wisaal duniya ko dekhte hue.

Ek patjhad ke din balloot naram tareeke se zameen par gira aur thandi mitti mein ludhak gaya. Wahan zameen ke gehre andar se ek purani jad ne use dheere se kaha. 'Nanhe, khud ko apne aakaar se mat naapo. Tumhare andar ek poora jungle soya hua hai — har tehni, har patta, har woh balloot jo abhi aana baaki hai.'

Balloot ko mushkil se yaqeen hua. Par shaant sardiyon mein woh aaram karta raha, aur basant mein ek komal hari kopal roshni ki taraf ubhri. Dheere-dheere, saal-dar-saal, woh badha. Aur ek din woh ooncha aur chauda khada tha, apne khud ke balloot giraate hue. Nanhe beej ne apna poora parivaar hamesha se apne andar liye rakha tha.

title.en: Tell your baby about the family they are being born into — the people who already love them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us parivaar ke baare mein bataayein jismein woh janm le raha hai — woh log jo abhi se unhe pyaar karte hain.

motivation.en: Your baby's first sense of belonging begins with hearing who they belong to.
motivation.hi: Aapke baby ke apnepan ka pehla ehsaas yeh sun kar shuru hota hai ki woh kiske hain.

description.en: A short meditation to feel held by family
description.hi: Parivaar se thaame hua mehsoos karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: You are surrounded by love, even before you arrive.
affirmation.hi: Tum pyaar se ghire hue ho, aane se pehle bhi.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and close your eyes. Take three slow breaths. Picture the people who love your baby gathered softly around you — and with each breath, let that warm circle of belonging settle over you both.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Teen dheemi saansein lein. Un logon ko sochein jo aapke baby ko pyaar karte hain, aapke aaspaas pyaar se ikatthe — aur har saans ke saath, apnepan ke us garm ghere ko aap dono par utarne dein.

closingThought.en: Your baby is already part of a circle that will hold them all their life.
closingThought.hi: Aapka baby pehle se ek aise ghere ka hissa hai jo unhe poori zindagi thaame rakhega.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

content.en: Sit with both feet flat on the floor. Breathe in slowly and imagine drawing steadiness up from the ground; breathe out and feel yourself settle. Repeat for one quiet minute.
content.hi: Dono pair farsh par seedhe rakh kar baithein. Dheere se saans lein aur sochein ki aap zameen se sthirta upar kheench rahi hain; saans chhodein aur khud ko jamta hua mehsoos karein. Ek shaant minute tak dohraayein.

remember.en: When you feel grounded, your baby does too.
remember.hi: Jab aap jami hui mehsoos karti hain, aapka baby bhi karta hai.

babyLearning.en: responding to sounds with little kicks
babyLearning.hi: aawaazon par nanhi laaton se jawaab de raha hai

insight.en: To belong somewhere — to be claimed and held by people — is one of the first and greatest gifts you can give your child.
insight.hi: Kahin ka hona — logon dwaara apnaaya aur thaama jaana — woh pehle aur sabse bade tohfon mein se ek hai jo aap apne bachche ko de sakti hain.

expanded.en: Long before your child can name it, they will feel whether they are wanted. Belonging is not something a child earns; it is something we give freely, simply because they are ours. The grandparents who already speak of them, the aunts and uncles who wait, the home being readied — all of this tells your baby, without a single word, that they have a place. That quiet certainty becomes the floor beneath everything they will ever try, risk and become. You are not just expecting a baby; a whole circle is widening to make room.
expanded.hi: Aapke bachche ke ise naam dene se bahut pehle, woh mehsoos karenge ki woh chaahe gaye hain ya nahi. Apnapan koi cheez nahi jo ek bachcha kamaata hai; yeh kuch aisa hai jo hum khulkar dete hain, sirf isliye ki woh hamare hain. Woh dadi-nani jo abhi se unki baat karte hain, woh chaacha-mausi jo intezaar karte hain, woh ghar jo taiyaar ho raha hai — yeh sab aapke baby ko, bina ek bhi shabd ke, batata hai ki unka ek sthaan hai. Woh shaant nishchitta woh farsh ban jaati hai jiske neeche woh sab kuch hai jo woh kabhi koshish karenge, jokhim lenge aur banenge. Aap sirf ek baby ka intezaar nahi kar rahin; ek poora ghera jagah banane ke liye phail raha hai.

deepDive.en: Child development experts note that a secure sense of belonging in the early years is strongly linked to confidence and emotional health later. Feeling 'part of us' is foundational, not optional.
deepDive.hi: Child development ke jaankaar bataate hain ki shuruaati saalon mein apnepan ki ek surakshit bhaavna baad ke aatmavishwaas aur emotional sehat se gehraai se judi hai. 'Hamara hissa' mehsoos karna buniyaadi hai, vaikalpik nahi.

remember.en: Belonging is given freely, never earned.
remember.hi: Apnapan khulkar diya jaata hai, kabhi kamaaya nahi jaata.

title.en: The Wise Bird And The Foolish Monkeys

summary.en: A little bird learns that advice is wasted on those who will not hear it.
summary.hi: Ek nanhi chidiya seekhti hai ki salah un par bekaar jaati hai jo use sunna hi nahi chahte.

body.en:
On a cold winter night, a troop of monkeys shivered under a tree, soaked by the rain. Nearby, a small bird sat warm and dry inside her snug, well-built nest. Watching the monkeys tremble, she felt sorry for them.

'Friends,' she called gently, 'why do you sit in the rain when you have such clever hands? You could weave a shelter of twigs and leaves, just as I built my nest, and keep yourselves warm.' She meant only to help.

But the monkeys grew angry at being advised by a tiny bird. 'Who are you to teach us how to live?' they snapped. In their temper they climbed up, tore her careful nest apart, and scattered it to the wind. The little bird flew off to build again elsewhere, having learned that wisdom offered to the unwilling is wisdom thrown away.
body.hi:
Ek thandi sardiyon ki raat, bandaron ka ek jhund ek ped ke neeche kaanp raha tha, baarish mein bheega hua. Paas hi, ek nanhi chidiya apne aaramdayak, achhe bane ghonsle mein garm aur sookhi baithi thi. Bandaron ko kaanpte dekh, use unpar taras aaya.

'Doston,' usne pyaar se pukaara, 'tumhare paas itne chalaak haath hain, phir baarish mein kyun baithe ho? Tum tinkon aur patton ka ek aashray bun sakte ho, bilkul jaise maine apna ghonsla banaaya, aur khud ko garm rakh sakte ho.' Uska matlab sirf madad karna tha.

Par bandaron ko ek nanhi chidiya se salah milne par gussa aa gaya. 'Tum kaun hoti ho humein jeena sikhaane waali?' woh bhadak gaye. Apne gusse mein woh upar chadhe, uske dhyan se bane ghonsle ko phaad daala, aur use hawa mein bikhraa diya. Nanhi chidiya kahin aur phir banane ke liye ud gayi, yeh seekh kar ki anichuk logon ko di gayi samajh, gawaayi gayi samajh hai.

title.en: Tell your baby about their grandparents and the way they already love them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko unke dada-dadi aur nana-nani ke baare mein bataayein, aur jis tarah woh abhi se unhe pyaar karte hain.

motivation.en: Speaking of the elders' love stitches your baby into a long line of belonging.
motivation.hi: Bujurgon ke pyaar ki baat karna aapke baby ko apnepan ki ek lambi kataar mein si deti hai.

description.en: Dawn raga for a fresh, hopeful beginning
description.hi: Bhor ka raag, ek nayi, ummeed bhari shuruaat ke liye

affirmation.en: As this dawn melody rises, a soft new hope rises in me, for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh bhor ki dhun uthti hai, mujhmein ek naram nayi ummeed uthti hai, tumhare liye.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

content.en: I carry the courage of everyone who came before me. I am not doing this alone, and I never was.
content.hi: Main un sabki himmat apne saath rakhti hoon jo mujhse pehle aaye. Main yeh akele nahi kar rahi, aur kabhi nahi thi.

remember.en: You are the latest in a long line of strong hearts.
remember.hi: Aap mazboot dilon ki ek lambi kataar mein sabse nayi hain.

babyLearning.en: turning toward your voice, learning your warmth
babyLearning.hi: aapki aawaz ki taraf mud raha hai, aapki garmjoshi seekh raha hai

insight.en: A child who feels deeply rooted is the one most free to explore. Belonging does not hold them back — it sets them free.
insight.hi: Jo bachcha gehraai se judey hue mehsoos karta hai wahi khojne ke liye sabse aazaad hota hai. Apnapan unhe rokta nahi — yeh unhe aazaad karta hai.

expanded.en: It can seem as if roots and freedom pull against each other — as if to hold a child close is to keep them small. But the opposite is true. A child who knows there is always a place to return to is the one brave enough to wander far. The security of home becomes the launchpad, not the cage. As you prepare your baby's place in the world, know that every bit of warmth and belonging you build is also building their future courage. Deep roots are what make tall branches possible.
expanded.hi: Aisa lag sakta hai jaise jadein aur aazaadi ek doosre ke khilaaf kheenchti hain — jaise bachche ko paas rakhna unhe chhota rakhna ho. Par sach iska ulta hai. Jo bachcha jaanta hai ki lautne ke liye hamesha ek jagah hai wahi itna himmati hota hai ki door tak ghoom sake. Ghar ki suraksha launchpad ban jaati hai, pinjra nahi. Jaise aap duniya mein apne baby ki jagah taiyaar karti hain, jaan lein ki aap jitni bhi garmjoshi aur apnapan banaati hain woh unki aage ki himmat bhi banata hai. Gehri jadein hi ooonchi tehniyon ko mumkin banaati hain.

deepDive.en: Attachment research is clear: children with a secure base — a sense that home is safe and steady — explore more boldly and recover from setbacks faster. Closeness builds courage, it does not weaken it.
deepDive.hi: Attachment research saaf hai: jin bachchon ka ek surakshit aadhaar hota hai — yeh ehsaas ki ghar surakshit aur sthir hai — woh zyada himmat se khojte hain aur thokron se jaldi sambhalte hain. Nazdeeki himmat banaati hai, use kamzor nahi karti.

remember.en: Deep roots are what let the branches reach high.
remember.hi: Gehri jadein hi tehniyon ko ooncha pahunchne deti hain.

summary.en: Baby Krishna grows up loved by two mothers, held by a double belonging.
summary.hi: Nanha Krishna do maaon ke pyaar mein bada hota hai, do apnepan se thaama hua.

body.en:
On the night Krishna was born, a great danger threatened him in the prison where his mother Devaki lay. To keep him safe, his father carried the newborn across a stormy river to the village of Gokul, where a loving couple, Nanda and Yashoda, took him in as their own.

So little Krishna grew up in Yashoda's home, cuddled and fed and sung to sleep, knowing nothing but love. Yashoda chased him through the courtyard, scolded him for stealing butter, and held him close each night. Far away, Devaki carried him always in her heart.

Krishna belonged to two mothers — one who gave him life and one who raised him — and both loved him completely. His story whispers a gentle truth: a child can be held by many hearts at once, and every loving arm becomes part of where they belong.
body.hi:
Jis raat Krishna ka janm hua, us jail mein ek bada khatra unpar mandraa raha tha jahaan unki maa Devaki padi thi. Use surakshit rakhne ke liye, unke pita navjaat ko ek toofani nadi paar Gokul gaon le gaye, jahaan ek pyaar karne wale dampati, Nanda aur Yashoda, ne use apna maan kar le liya.

To nanha Krishna Yashoda ke ghar mein bada hua, dulaara gaya, khilaaya gaya aur lori gaa kar sulaaya gaya, pyaar ke siva kuch na jaanta. Yashoda use aangan mein dhoondti, makhan churaane par daantti, aur har raat use paas thaame rakhti. Door, Devaki use hamesha apne dil mein liye rehti.

Krishna do maaon ke the — ek jisne unhe jeevan diya aur ek jisne unhe paala — aur dono unhe poori tarah pyaar karti theen. Unki kahani ek naram sachai dheere se kehti hai: ek bachche ko ek saath kayi dil thaame rakh sakte hain, aur har pyaar bhari baanh us jagah ka hissa ban jaati hai jahaan woh hain.

title.en: Tell your baby about the home that is waiting to welcome them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us ghar ke baare mein bataayein jo unhe apnaane ka intezaar kar raha hai.

motivation.en: Describing their home aloud begins building their first sense of safe ground.
motivation.hi: Unke ghar ko bolkar batana unki surakshit zameen ka pehla ehsaas banana shuru karta hai.

description.en: A short meditation to build a feeling of home
description.hi: Ghar ka ehsaas banane ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: Wherever we are, I am your home.
affirmation.hi: Hum jahaan bhi hon, main tumhara ghar hoon.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths and rest a hand on your belly. Imagine a warm, softly lit room inside you — safe, quiet, and waiting — and let your baby float gently in that feeling of home.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein aur ek haath pet par rakhein. Apne andar ek garm, halki roshni wala kamra sochein — surakshit, shaant, aur intezaar karta — aur apne baby ko ghar ke us ehsaas mein pyaar se tairne dein.

closingThought.en: Long before there are walls, you are already your baby's home.
closingThought.hi: Deewaaron ke hone se bahut pehle, aap pehle se hi apne baby ka ghar hain.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Jab Kuch Bhi Kaafi Na Lage

content.en: In these weeks your belly leaves little room, and big meals can feel too heavy. Try small, warm plates spread through the day — a bowl of khichdi, some dal and rice, soft fruit. Gentle and often is kinder to you both right now than large and rare.
content.hi: In hafton mein aapka pet kam jagah chhodta hai, aur bade khaane bahut bhaari lag sakte hain. Din bhar mein chhoti, garm thaaliyan baant kar try karein — ek katori khichdi, thoda dal-chawal, naram phal. Abhi naram aur baar-baar, bade aur kabhi-kabhaar se aap dono ke liye zyada dayaalu hai.

remember.en: Small and often beats large and rare these days.
remember.hi: In dinon chhota aur baar-baar, bade aur kabhi-kabhaar se behtar hai.

babyLearning.en: kicking and rolling, strong and lively now
babyLearning.hi: laaton aur karwaton se, ab mazboot aur chust

title.hi: Aap Peedhiyaan Apne Saath Rakhti Hain

insight.en: Inside you grows a child who carries the faces, gifts and stories of everyone who came before. They are a living thread of generations.
insight.hi: Aapke andar ek aisa bachcha badh raha hai jo un sabke chehre, hunar aur kahaaniyaan apne saath rakhta hai jo pehle aaye. Woh peedhiyon ka ek jeevit dhaaga hai.

expanded.en: Your baby is not beginning the story; they are its newest chapter. Somewhere in them may live a grandparent's laugh, a great-aunt's stubborn courage, a way of humming passed down without anyone teaching it. There is something deeply steadying in this — your child is not alone in the world, but woven into a long, living cloth of people. When you sense how much they carry forward, parenting feels less like building from nothing and more like passing on a flame that has been burning for a very long time.
expanded.hi: Aapka baby kahani shuru nahi kar raha; woh uska sabse naya adhyay hai. Unke andar kahin ek dada-dadi ki hansi, ek bujurg ki zid bhari himmat, gungunaane ka ek aisa tareeka jo bina kisi ke sikhaaye aage aaya — yeh sab bas sakta hai. Ismein kuch gehraai se sthir karne wala hai — aapka bachcha duniya mein akela nahi, balki logon ke ek lambe, jeevit kapde mein buna hua hai. Jab aap mehsoos karti hain ki woh kitna kuch aage le ja rahe hain, parenting bilkul shuruaat se banane se kam aur ek aisi lau ko aage badhane jaisa zyada lagta hai jo bahut lambe samay se jal rahi hai.

deepDive.en: Researchers find that children who hear stories of their ancestors and family roots develop a stronger 'intergenerational self' — a felt sense of being part of something enduring, which supports wellbeing.
deepDive.hi: Researchers paate hain ki jo bachche apne purvajon aur parivaar ki jadon ki kahaaniyaan sunte hain unmein ek mazboot 'intergenerational self' banta hai — kisi tikaau cheez ka hissa hone ka ek mehsoos hota ehsaas, jo bhalai mein madad karta hai.

remember.en: Your child is a living thread between past and future.
remember.hi: Aapka bachcha ateet aur bhavishya ke beech ek jeevit dhaaga hai.

summary.en: An old elephant's long memory guides the whole herd to safety.
summary.hi: Ek boodhe haathi ki lambi yaaddaasht poore jhund ko suraksha tak le jaati hai.

body.en:
In a great herd of elephants lived a very old matriarch, her skin creased like old bark. The young elephants sometimes grew impatient with her slow steps. 'Why do we follow one so old?' they grumbled among themselves.

Then came a terrible year with no rain. The rivers dried, the grass withered, and the herd began to weaken with thirst. The young ones did not know where to turn. But the old matriarch lifted her head and remembered — far back in her youth, in another dry year, her own mother had led the herd to a hidden valley with a spring that never failed.

Slowly, surely, she walked, and the whole herd followed. After many days she brought them to that very valley, still green, still watered. The young elephants understood at last: the memory of those who came before had carried them all to life.
body.hi:
Ek bade haathiyon ke jhund mein ek bahut boodhi mukhiya rehti thi, uski khaal purani chhaal jaisi jhurridaar. Jawaan haathi kabhi-kabhi uske dheere kadmon se bechain ho jaate. 'Hum itni boodhi ke peeche kyun chalein?' woh aapas mein bhunbhunaate.

Phir ek bhayaanak saal aaya jismein baarish nahi hui. Nadiyaan sookh gayeen, ghaas murjha gayi, aur jhund pyaas se kamzor padne laga. Jawaan haathi nahi jaante the ki kis taraf jaayein. Par boodhi mukhiya ne apna sir uthaaya aur use yaad aaya — bahut pehle apni jawaani mein, ek aur sookhe saal mein, uski apni maa ne jhund ko ek chhupi hui ghaati tak pahunchaaya tha jahaan ek aisa jharna tha jo kabhi nahi sookhta.

Dheere, mazbooti se, woh chali, aur poora jhund peeche chala. Kayi din baad woh unhe usi ghaati tak le aayi, abhi bhi hari, abhi bhi paani se bhari. Jawaan haathiyon ko aakhir samajh aaya: jo pehle aaye unki yaad ne un sabko zindagi tak pahunchaaya tha.

title.en: Tell your baby where your family comes from — the places and roots that made you.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aapka parivaar kahan se aata hai — woh jagahein aur jadein jinhone aapko banaaya.

motivation.en: Naming your roots aloud hands your baby a map of where they belong.
motivation.hi: Apni jadon ko bolkar batana aapke baby ko us jagah ka naksha thama deta hai jahaan woh hain.

title.en: I Pass On Love, Not Fear
title.hi: Main Pyaar Aage Deti Hoon, Dar Nahi

affirmation.en: From all that was passed to me, I choose to hand you the warmth, the courage and the love.
affirmation.hi: Jo kuch mujhe diya gaya, usmein se main tumhe garmjoshi, himmat aur pyaar dene ka chunaav karti hoon.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

content.en: Sit tall and breathe in slowly. As you breathe out, imagine warmth flowing gently down your spine, easing each tired muscle. Repeat for one slow minute.
content.hi: Seedhi baithein aur dheere se saans lein. Saans chhodte waqt, sochein ki garmjoshi pyaar se aapki reedh ke saath neeche beh rahi hai, har thaki maaspeshi ko aaram deti. Ek dheeme minute tak dohraayein.

remember.en: Your body carries a lot; let the breath unburden it.
remember.hi: Aapka sharir bahut kuch dhota hai; saans ko uska bojh halka karne dein.

babyLearning.hi: thamta, phir tan kar, aaram dhoondta

insight.en: Home is not a building or a place on a map. For your baby, home will be a feeling — the feeling of you.
insight.hi: Ghar koi imaarat ya naqshe par ek jagah nahi hai. Aapke baby ke liye, ghar ek ehsaas hoga — aapka ehsaas.

expanded.en: We often picture home as a house — rooms, walls, a familiar door. But long before your baby knows any of that, home will mean something far simpler: your heartbeat, your voice, the warmth of being held. Home is wherever they feel safe, and right now that home is entirely you. This is a comforting thing to remember in the busy work of preparing a nursery. The most important home you are making cannot be furnished or bought. It is made of your presence, and your baby already lives inside it.
expanded.hi: Hum aksar ghar ko ek makaan ki tarah sochte hain — kamre, deewaarein, ek jaana-pehchaana darwaaza. Par aapke baby ke ise jaanne se bahut pehle, ghar ka matlab kuch bahut saral hoga: aapki dhadkan, aapki aawaz, thaame jaane ki garmjoshi. Ghar wahan hai jahaan woh surakshit mehsoos karein, aur abhi woh ghar poori tarah aap hain. Nursery taiyaar karne ke vyast kaam mein yeh yaad rakhna sukoon dene wala hai. Sabse zaroori ghar jo aap bana rahi hain use sajaaya ya khareeda nahi ja sakta. Yeh aapki maujoodgi se bana hai, aur aapka baby pehle se hi uske andar rehta hai.

deepDive.en: Newborns recognise their mother's voice and scent within days, having learned them in the womb. The earliest sense of 'home' is sensory and relational, long before it is a physical place.
deepDive.hi: Navjaat shishu kuch hi dinon mein apni maa ki aawaz aur khushboo pehchaan lete hain, jise unhone garbh mein hi seekh liya tha. 'Ghar' ka sabse pehla ehsaas indriyon aur rishton ka hai, kisi bhautik jagah se bahut pehle.

remember.en: To your baby, you are home.
remember.hi: Aapke baby ke liye, aap hi ghar hain.

summary.en: Wise Birbal reveals a true son by listening for the voice of real love.
summary.hi: Samajhdaar Birbal sachche pyaar ki aawaz sun kar asli beta pehchaan leta hai.

body.en:
Two women came before Emperor Akbar's court, each claiming to be the mother of the same young boy. Each spoke with tears and certainty, and no one could tell which was telling the truth. The puzzled emperor turned, as he often did, to clever Birbal.

Birbal listened quietly, then announced a solution. 'Since we cannot decide, the boy shall be divided fairly between you — half to each.' He raised a hand as if to give the order.

At once one woman cried out, 'No! Please, do not harm him. Let her keep him — only let him live!' The other stayed silent. Birbal smiled gently and pointed to the woman who had pleaded. 'There stands his true mother. Only real love would rather give a child away than see him hurt.' And so the boy was returned to the heart that truly held him.
body.hi:
Do auratein Badshah Akbar ke darbaar mein aayeen, har ek usi jawaan ladke ki maa hone ka daava karti. Dono aansuon aur yaqeen ke saath bolti theen, aur koi nahi bata sakta tha ki kaun sach keh rahi hai. Uljhe hue badshah ne, jaisa woh aksar karta, chalaak Birbal ki taraf rukh kiya.

Birbal ne chupchaap suna, phir ek hal sunaaya. 'Jab hum faisla nahi kar sakte, to ladke ko aap dono ke beech barabar baant diya jaaye — aadha har ek ko.' Usne hukm dene ke andaaz mein haath uthaaya.

Turant ek aurat chillaayi, 'Nahi! Kripya, use chot mat pahunchaao. Use ise rakhne do — bas ise jeene do!' doosri chup rahi. Birbal pyaar se muskuraaya aur us aurat ki taraf ishaara kiya jisne vinti ki thi. 'Wahan khadi hai uski asli maa. Sirf sachcha pyaar hi bachche ko khone dega bajaaye use chot lagte dekhne ke.' Aur is tarah ladka us dil ko laut diya gaya jo use sach mein thaame tha.

title.en: Tell your baby about a place that has always felt like home to you.
title.hi: Apne baby ko ek aisi jagah ke baare mein bataayein jo aapko hamesha ghar jaisi lagi hai.

motivation.en: Sharing what home feels like to you teaches your baby the shape of belonging.
motivation.hi: Aapko ghar kaisa lagta hai yeh share karna aapke baby ko apnepan ka aakaar sikhaata hai.

description.en: Evening raga for warmth and ease
description.hi: Shaam ka raag, garmjoshi aur sukoon ke liye

affirmation.en: As this evening melody softens, my whole body softens, around you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh shaam ki dhun naram hoti hai, mera poora sharir naram hota hai, tumhare around.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.hi: Main Ghar Apne Andar Rakhti Hoon

content.en: I do not need everything to be perfect to feel at home. I carry calm and belonging within me, wherever I am.
content.hi: Ghar jaisa mehsoos karne ke liye mujhe sab kuch perfect hone ki zaroorat nahi. Main shaanti aur apnapan apne andar rakhti hoon, main jahaan bhi hoon.

remember.en: Home is something you carry, not only somewhere you go.
remember.hi: Ghar woh hai jo aap apne saath rakhti hain, sirf koi jagah nahi jahaan aap jaati hain.

babyLearning.en: listening close, learning the voices around
babyLearning.hi: dhyan se sun raha hai, aaspaas ki aawaazein seekh raha hai

insight.en: One of the kindest things you can give your child is their story — where they began, and the people who waited for them.
insight.hi: Sabse dayaalu cheezon mein se ek jo aap apne bachche ko de sakti hain woh hai unki kahani — woh kahan se shuru hue, aur woh log jinhone unka intezaar kiya.

expanded.en: Every child wonders, sooner or later, 'Where did I come from? Whose am I?' Long before they can ask, you can begin gathering the answers — the small stories of how they were waited for, who held the news with joy, what the family hoped for them. These are not grand tales; they are the simple threads of belonging. A child who grows up knowing their story carries a quiet confidence: they know they did not appear by accident, but arrived into open and waiting arms.
expanded.hi: Har bachcha jaldi ya der se sochta hai, 'Main kahan se aaya? Main kiska hoon?' Unke poochhne se bahut pehle, aap jawaab ikatthe karna shuru kar sakti hain — woh chhoti kahaaniyaan ki unka kaise intezaar kiya gaya, kisne khushi se khabar thaami, parivaar ne unke liye kya ummeed ki. Yeh koi badi gaathaayein nahi; yeh apnepan ke saral dhaage hain. Jo bachcha apni kahani jaante hue bada hota hai woh ek shaant aatmavishwaas rakhta hai: woh jaanta hai ki woh galti se nahi aaya, balki khuli aur intezaar karti baahon mein aaya.

deepDive.en: Family researchers found that children who could answer questions about their family history scored higher on measures of resilience and self-esteem. Knowing one's story is quietly protective.
deepDive.hi: Family researchers ne paaya ki jo bachche apne parivaar ke itihaas ke sawaalon ka jawaab de sakte the woh lachilepan aur self-esteem ke paimaanon par zyada score karte the. Apni kahani jaanna chupchaap raksha karta hai.

remember.en: A child who knows their story stands a little taller.
remember.hi: Jo bachcha apni kahani jaanta hai woh thoda seedha khada hota hai.

summary.en: A pair of swallows journey across the world and return to the very same eave.
summary.hi: Ababeelon ka ek joda duniya bhar safar karta hai aur usi chhajje par laut aata hai.

body.en:
Under the eaves of an old village house, two swallows built a small mud nest each spring. There they raised their chicks, who grew strong and learned to fly above the rooftops. When the cold months came, the whole little family rose into the sky and flew far away, over hills and rivers and seas, to warmer lands.

The young swallows saw wondrous places on their long journey. They wheeled over golden deserts and shining coasts. Yet when spring returned, something deep inside them turned them homeward, across all those miles, back to the very same village, the very same house, the very same eave.

The old farmer below smiled to see them return. 'No matter how far they wander,' he said softly, 'they always know where home is.' For some places stay written in the heart, calling us gently back, however far we roam.
body.hi:
Ek purane gaon ke ghar ke chhajje ke neeche, do ababeelein har basant ek chhota mitti ka ghonsla banaati theen. Wahan woh apne bachchon ko paalti, jo mazboot hote aur chhaton ke upar udna seekhte. Jab thande mahine aate, poora chhota parivaar aasmaan mein uthta aur door ud jaata, pahaadon, nadiyon aur samundron ke paar, garm dharti tak.

Jawaan ababeelein apne lambe safar mein adbhut jagahein dekhteen. Woh sunhre registaano aur chamakte tat ke upar mandraateen. Phir bhi jab basant lauta, unke andar gehraai mein kuch unhe ghar ki taraf moda, un sab meelon ke paar, wapas usi gaon, usi ghar, usi chhajje tak.

Neeche boodha kisaan unhe lautte dekh muskuraaya. 'Woh chaahe kitni bhi door ghoom lein,' usne dheere se kaha, 'unhe hamesha pata hota hai ki ghar kahan hai.' Kyunki kuch jagahein dil mein likhi reh jaati hain, humein pyaar se wapas bulaati, hum chaahe kitna bhi ghoom lein.

title.en: Tell your baby about the wider community that will come to know and love them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us bade samuday ke baare mein bataayein jo unhe jaanega aur pyaar karega.

motivation.en: Your baby belongs not only to a family but to a whole circle of people.
motivation.hi: Aapka baby sirf ek parivaar ka nahi, balki logon ke ek poore ghere ka hai.

affirmation.en: You are not coming to us as a stranger. You are already one of us, woven into our story.
affirmation.hi: Tum hamare paas kisi ajnabi ki tarah nahi aa rahe. Tum pehle se hi hamare ek ho, hamari kahani mein bune hue.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

content.en: As the weeks go on, that rising burn after meals can be tiring. It often helps to eat a little less at a time, stay upright for a while after eating, and lean on soothing foods like cool milk, soft bananas or a little curd. Be gentle with your evenings.
content.hi: Jaise hafte aage badhte hain, khaane ke baad uthti woh jalan thaka deti hai. Aksar madad karta hai ki ek baar mein thoda kam khaayein, khaane ke baad kuch der seedha rahein, aur thandi doodh, naram kele ya thoda dahi jaise sukoon dene wale khaane ka sahaara lein. Apni shaamon ke saath naram rahein.

remember.en: Smaller meals and a little patience ease the evenings.
remember.hi: Chhote khaane aur thoda sabra shaamon ko aasaan kar dete hain.

babyLearning.en: closing the week, strong and kicking
babyLearning.hi: hafta poora karte hue, mazboot aur laaten maarte hue

insight.en: More than anything, your child will need to know there is one place, one heart, they can always return to. That place is you.
insight.hi: Sab se zyada, aapke bachche ko yeh jaanne ki zaroorat hogi ki ek jagah hai, ek dil hai, jahaan woh hamesha laut sakte hain. Woh jagah aap hain.

expanded.en: As this week of roots and belonging draws to a close, hold on to one simple truth: your child does not need a perfect home, a perfect parent or a perfect start. They need a place to always return to — arms that open the same way every time, a love that does not depend on how the day went. You are building that place now, in your steadiness and your warmth. Whatever they grow into, however far they go, they will carry the quiet knowing that somewhere, someone is always glad they came home.
expanded.hi: Jaise jadon aur apnepan ka yeh hafta khatam hota hai, ek saral sachai thaame rakhein: aapke bachche ko ek perfect ghar, ek perfect parent ya ek perfect shuruaat ki zaroorat nahi. Unhe hamesha lautne ki ek jagah chahiye — woh baahein jo har baar usi tarah khulein, ek aisa pyaar jo is par nirbhar na ho ki din kaisa raha. Aap woh jagah abhi bana rahi hain, apni sthirta aur apni garmjoshi mein. Woh jo bhi banein, jitni bhi door jaayein, woh yeh shaant jaan apne saath rakhenge ki kahin, koi hamesha khush hai ki woh ghar laut aaye.

deepDive.en: Decades of research on the 'secure base' show that a child who trusts there is always someone to return to grows more confident and emotionally steady. Reliable return matters more than constant presence.
deepDive.hi: 'Secure base' par dashakon ki research dikhaati hai ki jo bachcha bharosa karta hai ki lautne ke liye hamesha koi hai woh zyada aatmavishwaasi aur emotional roop se sthir hota hai. Bharosemand wapsi, lagaataar maujoodgi se zyada maayne rakhti hai.

remember.en: Be the place your child can always come home to.
remember.hi: Woh jagah banein jahaan aapka bachcha hamesha ghar laut sake.

summary.en: A single seed learns it was never alone — a whole forest holds it.
summary.hi: Ek akela beej seekhta hai ki woh kabhi akela nahi tha — ek poora jungle use thaame hai.

body.en:
A small seed lay buried in the dark earth and felt utterly alone. 'Here I am,' it thought, 'tiny and lost, with no one near.' It did not know that all around it, beneath the soil, ran a vast and silent web of roots — the roots of every tree in the forest, woven together underground.

As the seed sent down its first thin root, it touched the older roots nearby. Quietly, the great trees began to share with it — a little water through their tangled threads, a little nourishment, a steadiness passed from root to root. The tiny seed was being held by the whole forest, though it could not see a thing.

In time it grew into a strong young tree, joined to all the others below. And it understood at last: no seed in a forest ever truly grows alone. It belongs, from its very first day, to everything around it.
body.hi:
Ek chhota beej andheri zameen mein dafan pada tha aur khud ko bilkul akela mehsoos karta tha. 'Yahan main hoon,' usne socha, 'nanha aur kho gaya, koi paas nahi.' Use nahi pata tha ki uske chaaron taraf, mitti ke neeche, jadon ka ek vishaal aur shaant jaal phaila tha — jungle ke har ped ki jadein, zameen ke neeche ek saath buni hui.

Jaise beej ne apni pehli patli jad neeche bheji, usne paas ki purani jadon ko chhua. Chupchaap, bade ped uske saath baantne lage — apne uljhe dhaagon se thoda paani, thoda poshan, ek se doosri jad tak pohanchti ek sthirta. Nanha beej poore jungle dwaara thaama ja raha tha, halaanki use kuch dikhaayi nahi de raha tha.

Samay ke saath woh ek mazboot jawaan ped bana, neeche baaki sab se juda hua. Aur use aakhir samajh aaya: jungle mein koi beej kabhi sach mein akela nahi badhta. Woh apne pehle hi din se, apne aaspaas ki har cheez ka hota hai.

title.en: Tell your baby how, even now, they already belong — completely and without condition.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki kaise, abhi bhi, woh pehle se apne hain — poori tarah aur bina kisi shart ke.

motivation.en: The truest gift you can offer is the certainty that they belong.
motivation.hi: Sabse sachcha tohfa jo aap de sakti hain woh yeh nishchitta hai ki woh apne hain.

description.en: A short meditation on safe return
description.hi: Surakshit wapsi par ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: Wherever you go, you will always find your way back to me.
affirmation.hi: Tum jahaan bhi jaao, tum hamesha mujh tak wapas raasta paa loge.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and close your eyes. Take three slow breaths. Rest a hand on your belly and silently make a promise: that you will always be a safe place to return to. Let that quiet vow settle warmly between you and your baby.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Teen dheemi saansein lein. Ek haath pet par rakhein aur mann hi mann ek vaada karein: ki aap hamesha lautne ki ek surakshit jagah rahengi. Us shaant vaade ko aapke aur aapke baby ke beech garmjoshi se basne dein.

closingThought.en: You have given your baby the deepest gift of all — a place to always belong.
closingThought.hi: Aapne apne baby ko sabse gehra tohfa diya hai — hamesha apne hone ki ek jagah.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

content.en: Breathe in slowly and silently say 'I am held.' Breathe out and say 'You are held.' Let the two of you rest in that exchange for one quiet minute.
content.hi: Dheere se saans lein aur mann hi mann kahein 'main thaami hui hoon.' Saans chhodein aur kahein 'tum thaame hue ho.' Aap dono ko ek shaant minute tak us aadan-pradaan mein aaram karne dein.

remember.en: When you feel held, you hold your baby more gently.
remember.hi: Jab aap thaami hui mehsoos karti hain, aap apne baby ko aur narmi se thaamti hain.
